16	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
17	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
18	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
19	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
20	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
21	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
22		cmd := &cobra.Command{
23			Use:   "work",
24			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
25			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
26	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
27	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
28	
29	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, --model, and --profile as opaque
30	passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate
31	these values or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
32	bounds.
33	
34	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
35	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
36	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
37	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
38	
39	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
40	  drained     — no ready beads remain
41	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
42	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
43	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
44	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
45	
46	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
47	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
48	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
49	`,
50			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
51	  ddx work
52	
53	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
54	  ddx work --once
55	
56	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
57	  ddx work --watch
58	
59	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
60	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
61	
62	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
63	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
64	
65	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
66	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
67	
68	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
69	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
70			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
71			RunE: f.runWork,
72		}
73

117	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
118	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
119	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
120	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
121	// by this layer.
122	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
123		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
124			return err
125		}
126	
127		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
128		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
129	
130		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
131		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
132		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
133		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
134		if !asJSON {
135			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
136			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
137				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
138			})
139		}
140	
141		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
142	}

144	func writeExecuteLoopResult(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult, asJSON bool) error {
145		if asJSON {
146			payload := struct {
147				ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
148				*agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult
149			}{
150				ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
151				ExecuteBeadLoopResult: result,
152			}
153			enc := json.NewEncoder(w)
154			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
155			return enc.Encode(payload)
156		}
157	
158		if result.NoReadyWork && result.Attempts == 0 {
159			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, projectRoot, result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
160			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
161			return nil
162		}
163	
164		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nproject: %s\n", projectRoot)
165		writeWorkTerminalSummary(w, result)
166		if result.Failures > 0 {
167			fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nfailed:\n")
168			for _, attempt := range result.Results {
169				if attempt.Status != "success" {
170					fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - %s: %s", attempt.BeadID, attempt.Detail)
171					if attempt.PreserveRef != "" {
172						fmt.Fprintf(w, " (preserved)")
173					}
174					fmt.Fprintln(w)
175				}
176			}
177		}
178		if result.NoReadyWork {
179			fmt.Fprintln(w)
180			writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w, "", result.NoReadyWorkDetail)
181			writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w, result.QueueSnapshot)
182		}
183		return nil
184	}

186	func writeWorkTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) {
187		closed, changed, alreadySatisfied := countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result)
188		fmt.Fprintf(w, "worker exited: %s\n", workExitSummary(result))
189		fmt.Fprintf(w, "attempts: %d  |  closed: %d  |  changed: %d  |  already-satisfied: %d  |  failures: %d\n",
190			result.Attempts, closed, changed, alreadySatisfied, result.Failures)
191	}
192	
193	func countWorkTerminalOutcomes(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) (closed, changed, alreadySatisfied int) {
194		for _, attempt := range result.Results {
195			switch attempt.Status {
196			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
197				closed++
198				changed++
199			case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
200				closed++
201				alreadySatisfied++
202			}
203		}
204		if closed == 0 && result.Successes > 0 {
205			closed = result.Successes
206		}
207		return closed, changed, alreadySatisfied
208	}
209	
210	func workExitSummary(result *agent.ExecuteBeadLoopResult) string {
211		switch result.ExitReason {
212		case "drained":
213			return "drained current execution-ready queue"
214		case "once_complete":
215			return "once complete"
216		case "budget":
217			return "budget reached"
218		case "no_progress":
219			return "no-progress policy stopped work"
220		case "blocked":
221			return "blocked waiting for external action"
222		case "sigint", "sigterm", "context_cancelled":
223			return "stopped by signal"
224		case "fatal_config":
225			return "fatal configuration error"
226		case "preflight_failed":
227			return "preflight failed"
228		case "resource_exhausted":
229			return "resource exhausted"
230		case "routing_unavailable":
231			return "routing unavailable"
232		}
233		if result.StopCondition != "" {
234			return strings.ToLower(strings.ReplaceAll(result.StopCondition, "_", " "))
235		}
236		return "completed"
237	}

239	func writeQueueSnapshotTerminalSummary(w io.Writer, snapshot *agent.QueueSnapshot) {
240		if snapshot == nil {
241			return
242		}
243		retry := ""
244		if snapshot.NextRetryAfter != "" {
245			retry = " next-retry=" + snapshot.NextRetryAfter
246		}
247		fmt.Fprintf(w, "remaining queue: execution-ready=%d blocked=%d operator-attention=%d needs-human/investigation=%d cooldown/deferred=%d%s execution-ineligible=%d superseded=%d epics=%d epic-closure-candidates=%d\n",
248			snapshot.ExecutionReadyCount,
249			snapshot.BlockedCount,
250			snapshot.ProposedOperatorAttentionCount,
251			snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount,
252			snapshot.RetryCooldownCount,
253			retry,
254			snapshot.ExecutionIneligibleCount,
255			snapshot.SupersededCount,
256			snapshot.SkippedEpicsCount,
257			snapshot.EpicClosureCandidatesCount,
258		)
259		if snapshot.HumanReviewBlockedTotal <= 0 || snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount <= 0 {
260			return
261		}
262		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%d beads blocked behind %d needs-human blockers:\n",
263			snapshot.HumanReviewBlockedTotal, snapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount)
264		for i, blocker := range snapshot.HumanReviewBlockers {
265			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  %d. %s %s (%d downstream)\n", i+1, blocker.ID, blocker.Title, blocker.DownstreamBlockedCount)
266		}
267	}

269	func writeNoReadyWorkSummary(w io.Writer, projectRoot string, d agent.NoReadyWorkBreakdown) {
270		if projectRoot != "" {
271			fmt.Fprintf(w, "project: %s\n", projectRoot)
272		}
273		fmt.Fprintln(w, "No execution-ready beads.")
274		if noReadyWorkBreakdownEmpty(d) {
275			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  queue drained: no open work remains in lifecycle queues.")
276		}
277		if len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention) > 0 {
278			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  operator attention: %d proposed bead(s) stop autonomous work and may block downstream dependents: %s\n",
279				len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention), strings.Join(d.ProposedOperatorAttention, ", "))
280		}
281		if len(d.DependencyWaiting) > 0 {
282			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  waiting on dependencies: %d open bead(s): %s\n",
283				len(d.DependencyWaiting), strings.Join(d.DependencyWaiting, ", "))
284		}
285		if len(d.ExternalBlocked) > 0 {
286			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  external blocked: %d bead(s) with explicit blocked status: %s\n",
287				len(d.ExternalBlocked), strings.Join(d.ExternalBlocked, ", "))
288		}
289		if len(d.RetryCooldown) > 0 {
290			retryHint := ""
291			if d.NextRetryAfter != "" {
292				retryHint = " (next retry-after: " + d.NextRetryAfter + ")"
293			}
294			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  retry cooldown: %d bead(s)%s: %s\n",
295				len(d.RetryCooldown), retryHint, strings.Join(d.RetryCooldown, ", "))
296		}
297		if len(d.NotEligible) > 0 {
298			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  not execution eligible: %d bead(s): %s\n",
299				len(d.NotEligible), strings.Join(d.NotEligible, ", "))
300		}
301		if len(d.Superseded) > 0 {
302			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  superseded: %d bead(s): %s\n",
303				len(d.Superseded), strings.Join(d.Superseded, ", "))
304		}
305		if len(d.Epics) > 0 {
306			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  epic containers: %d ready epic(s) with open children (decompose into tasks): %s\n",
307				len(d.Epics), strings.Join(d.Epics, ", "))
308		}
309		if len(d.EpicClosureCandidates) > 0 {
310			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  completed epic closure candidate(s) (all direct children closed; surfaced for closure evaluation): %s\n",
311				strings.Join(d.EpicClosureCandidates, ", "))
312		}
313	}
314	
315	func noReadyWorkBreakdownEmpty(d agent.NoReadyWorkBreakdown) bool {
316		return len(d.ExecutionReady) == 0 &&
317			len(d.DependencyWaiting) == 0 &&
318			len(d.ProposedOperatorAttention) == 0 &&
319			len(d.RetryCooldown) == 0 &&
320			len(d.ExternalBlocked) == 0 &&
321			len(d.NotEligible) == 0 &&
322			len(d.Superseded) == 0 &&
323			len(d.Epics) == 0 &&
324			len(d.EpicClosureCandidates) == 0 &&
325			d.NextRetryAfter == ""
326	}

1	# Status — Queue State and Health
2	
3	## Mode note
4	
5	This reference covers **direct queue queries and health checks** (`queue_steward`
6	and `interactive-steward` orient phase). Use it for:
7	
8	- "What's on the queue?" "What's ready?" "Is DDx healthy?" "Sync status?"
9	
10	If the user is asking a **broad planning or orientation question** — "what should
11	I work on next?", "what's blocking the queue?" — see `reference/interactive.md`
12	first. The interactive steward uses these commands to build `QueueFacts` but
13	does not instruct manual ready-bead implementation; `ddx work` is the queue
14	worker.
15	
16	---
17	
18	Three distinct intents live here. They answer different questions
19	and have different commands; call them by intent, not
20	interchangeably.
21	
22	## What's on the queue?
23	
24	"What's ready to work on?" "What's blocked?"
25	
26	```bash
27	ddx bead ready      # execution-ready open beads; use --execution for worker view
28	ddx bead blocked    # status=blocked external/recheckable blockers
29	ddx bead status     # aggregate lifecycle and derived queue counts
30	ddx bead list       # all beads (filter with --status, --label, --where)
31	```
32	
33	Typical answers:
34	
35	- "What should I work on next?" → See `reference/interactive.md` for the
36	  interactive-steward planning workflow. The steward uses `ddx bead ready` to
37	  build `QueueFacts` but will not instruct you to manually implement ready beads.
38	- "Why isn't bead X moving?" → `ddx bead show <id>` for full state;
39	  `ddx bead dep tree <id>` to see what it depends on.
40	- "How many beads are open right now?" → `ddx bead status`.
41	- "What is waiting on dependencies?" → `ddx bead ready` / `ddx bead status`;
42	  dependency waits are derived queue state, not `status=blocked`.
43	
44	## Am I healthy?
45	
46	"Is DDx installed and working?" "Are the harnesses available?"
47	
48	```bash
49	ddx doctor          # environment, config, install validation
50	# Fizeau diagnostics, if exposed by the project, remain separate from DDx.
51	ddx version         # CLI version
52	```
53	
54	`ddx doctor` checks:
55	
56	- DDx binary is reachable and recent.
57	- `.ddx/config.yaml` is valid (schema + required fields).
58	- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` is readable and parses.
59	- Git is available and the repo is in a usable state.
60	- Shell integration (where applicable) is set up.
61	- Plugins declared in config are actually installed.
62	
63	Fizeau diagnostics go further into the execution service: which
64	harnesses are discoverable, whether provider credentials are present,
65	and whether routing metadata loads.
66	
67	## Is the project drifted from upstream?
68	
69	"Has the DDx library changed since I last updated?" "Do I need to
70	pull new plugin content?"
71	
72	```bash
73	ddx status          # sync state: upstream revs, local drift, stale docs
74	ddx doc stale       # documents that reference outdated artifacts
75	```
76	
77	`ddx status` is the CLI-wide status: binary version, plugin
78	versions, document-graph freshness. `ddx doc stale` specifically
79	lists documents that depend on artifacts that have moved or
80	changed.
81	
82	## Don't use these interchangeably
83	
84	- `ddx doctor` — **environment health** (is DDx working on this
85	  machine?). Call after `ddx upgrade` or when things seem broken.
86	- Fizeau diagnostics — **harness health** (are the routes reachable?).
87	  Call when `ddx run` reports an availability failure.
88	- `ddx status` — **upstream drift** (is this project in sync with
89	  the library registry?). Call periodically to catch stale
90	  plugins.
91	- `ddx bead status` — **work queue state** (how many beads, in
92	  what states?). Call to understand project progress.
93	
94	A user asking "how's the project doing?" probably means `ddx
95	doctor` or the Fizeau diagnostics surface (am I set up correctly) —
96	follow up with `ddx bead status` if they want work-queue state too.
97	
98	A user asking "what's ready to work on?" is clearly asking for
99	`ddx bead ready`.
100	
101	## Anti-patterns
102	
103	- **Running `ddx status` to check if routes work.** Use
104	  the Fizeau diagnostics surface for that; `ddx status` is about upstream sync.
105	- **Running `ddx doctor` to see the queue.** Use `ddx bead status`.
106	- **Closing stale beads based on "status" alone.** `ddx bead
107	  status` shows counts, not quality. Use `ddx bead list
108	  --status open` + `ddx bead show <id>` to actually read each bead.
109	
110	## CLI reference
111	
112	```bash
113	# Queue state
114	ddx bead ready
115	ddx bead blocked
116	ddx bead status
117	ddx bead list [--status open|closed] [--label <l>]
118	ddx bead show <id>
119	
120	# Environment health
121	ddx doctor
122	ddx version
123	
124	# Agent/harness health
125	# Fizeau diagnostics, if exposed by the project, remain separate from DDx.
126	
127	# Upstream sync
128	ddx status
129	ddx doc stale
130	```
131	
132	Full flag list: `ddx doctor --help`,
133	`ddx bead --help`.
134

1	# Work — Drain the Queue, Execute, Verify, Close
2	
3	## Mode note
4	
5	This reference covers **`bead_execution` mode**: explicit worker commands such as
6	`ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", or "start the worker".
7	
8	If the user said "what should I work on next?", "what's blocking the queue?", or
9	"work the queue" without an explicit bead ID or `ddx work` command, they are in
10	**`interactive-steward`** mode — see `reference/interactive.md`. The steward
11	plans; the worker executes. Do not instruct manual ready-bead implementation in
12	response to broad orientation prompts.
13	
14	---
15	
16	"Doing work" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,
17	run one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on
18	success or leave it available for a future eligible retry.
19	
20	## Default surface: `ddx work`
21	
22	```bash
23	ddx work
24	```
25	
26	`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.
27	`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single agent invocation primitive. DDx
28	owns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; Fizeau owns concrete
29	routing, provider/model discovery, alias resolution, fuzzy matching, catalog
30	lookups, and transcript/session rendering.
31	
32	Flags worth knowing:
33	
34	- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).
35	- `--watch` — keep running after the current ready queue drains; wait for newly-ready beads.
36	- `--idle-interval <dur>` — sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode (default 30s).
37	- `--min-power <n>` / `--max-power <n>` — requested agent power bounds.
38	- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the service's reported
39	  power bands.
40	- `--harness <name>` / `--provider <name>` / `--model <ref>` / `--profile <name>` — passthrough constraints only. DDx sends them
41	  unchanged and does not route on them.
42	
43	## Primitive: `ddx try`
44	
45	For targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:
46	
47	```bash
48	ddx try <bead-id>
49	ddx try <bead-id> --from <rev>      # base commit override
50	ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land
51	```
52	
53	`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`
54	for the actual agent invocation.
55	
56	## Pick by default, not by ID
57	
58	Under normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks
59	the top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only
60	pin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try <id>`) or
61	when the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to
62	override.
63	
64	## Verify independently before closing
65	
66	**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the
67	agent's self-report. Before closing a bead:
68	
69	1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's
70	   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.
71	2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from
72	   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the
73	   attempt.
74	3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?
75	   (`[ddx-<id>]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)
76	
77	If all three pass: close the bead.
78	
79	## Close on success, unclaim on failure
80	
81	DDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome
82	maps to a concrete follow-up action:
83	
84	| Outcome | Meaning | Action |
85	|---|---|---|
86	| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close <id>` |
87	| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close <id>` |
88	| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |
89	| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |
90	| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |
91	| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
92	| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |
93	| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
94	
95	`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running
96	`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update <id>
97	--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another
98	worker can pick it up.
99	
100	**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or
101	unclaims.
102	
103	## Testing expectations for bead implementations
104	
105	When an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests
106	that exercise the new code:
107	
108	- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party
109	  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call
110	  sequences test implementation, not behavior.
111	- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost
112	  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real
113	  HTTP via a local test server).
114	- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock
115	  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their
116	  scope.
117	- **Coverage measurement only where the project tracks it.** Don't
118	  introduce coverage tooling just for one bead; use what the
119	  project already has. Coverage is a signal, not a target.
120	- **Performance claims require baselines.** If the bead's AC
121	  mentions "faster" or "scales", the acceptance test must include:
122	  (a) a numeric baseline, (b) an explicit boundary (what's
123	  measured, what's excluded), (c) a reproducible harness.
124	
125	## Anti-patterns
126	
127	- **Trusting the agent's "done"**: always re-run the AC command
128	  yourself before closing.
129	- **Closing on no_changes/no_evidence**: only `success` and `already_satisfied`
130	  close a bead. `no_changes` requires an explicit rationale. `no_evidence_produced`
131	  means the agent returned without a commit or rationale — unclaim and investigate.
132	- **Squashing bead-attempt commits**: the per-attempt history is
133	  an audit trail (evidence commits, heartbeats). Use only
134	  `git merge --ff-only` or `--no-ff`; never squash/rebase/filter.
135	- **Running passthrough pins without a reason**: power-bound dispatch lets the
136	  agent choose an appropriate route. Use `--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`,
137	  or `--profile` only for explicit operator constraints, bug
138	  repros, or controlled tests.
139	- **Parallel workers on the same claimed bead**: the tracker
140	  guards against this via claim semantics, but don't try to defeat
141	  it — each claim represents an in-flight attempt.
142	
143	## CLI reference
144	
145	```bash
146	ddx work                                    # default queue drain
147	ddx work --once                             # one bead, then stop
148	ddx work --watch                            # continuous worker
149	ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s        # shorter idle scan interval
150	ddx work --min-power 10                     # request stronger attempts
151	ddx work --harness claude                   # passthrough constraint
152	ddx work --profile default                  # passthrough constraint
153	
154	ddx try <id>                                # one bead attempt
155	ddx try <id> --from <rev>                   # override base commit
156	ddx try <id> --no-merge                     # preserve iteration
157	```
158	
159	Full flag list: `ddx work --help`, `ddx try --help`, `ddx run --help`.

374		// Use a generous safety-net timeout so the race detector's overhead

377		// check below, which catches the bug (ddx work hanging on empty queue).

429	func (f *CommandFactory) displayPluginStalenessHints(cmd *cobra.Command) {
430		state, err := registry.LoadState()
431		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
432			return
433		}
434	
435		cache := update.NewPluginCache()
436		if err := cache.Load(); err != nil {
437			return // no cache yet — check runs in background, hints appear next time
438		}
439	
440		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
441			latestVersion, ok := cache.Data.Plugins[entry.Name]
442			if !ok {
443				continue
444			}
445			needsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(entry.Version, latestVersion)
446			if err == nil && needsUpgrade {
447				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),
448					"\n💡 %s %s installed, %s available. Run 'ddx install %s' to update.\n",
449					entry.Name, entry.Version, latestVersion, entry.Name)
450			}

454	// refreshPluginVersionCache fetches the latest version for each installed plugin
455	// from GitHub and writes the result to the plugin cache. Called from the
456	// background update goroutine — failures are silent.
457	func (f *CommandFactory) refreshPluginVersionCache() {
458		cache := update.NewPluginCache()
459		_ = cache.Load() // ignore missing-file error
460	
461		if !cache.IsExpired() {
462			return
463		}
464	
465		state, err := registry.LoadState()
466		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
467			return
468		}
469	
470		reg := registry.BuiltinRegistry()
471		updated := false
472		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
473			pkg, err := reg.Find(entry.Name)
474			if err != nil {
475				continue
476			}
477			release, err := update.FetchLatestReleaseForRepo(pkg.Source)
478			if err != nil {
479				continue
480			}
481			latestVersion := strings.TrimPrefix(release.TagName, "v")
482			cache.Data.Plugins[entry.Name] = latestVersion
483			updated = true
484		}
485	
486		if updated {
487			cache.Data.LastCheck = time.Now()
488			_ = cache.Save()
489		}
490	}

592	func (f *CommandFactory) warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd *cobra.Command) {
593		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
594			return
595		}
596		if f.Commit == "" || f.Commit == "unknown" {
597			return
598		}
599	
600		repoRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
601		if repoRoot == "" || !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
602			return
603		}
604	
605		originURL, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "remote", "get-url", "origin")
606		if err != nil || !isDDXOriginURL(originURL) {
607			return
608		}
609	
610		buildSHA, err := gitResolveCommit(repoRoot, f.Commit)
611		if err != nil {
612			return
613		}
614		headSHA, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
615		if err != nil {
616			return
617		}
618	
619		if err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), repoRoot, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", buildSHA, headSHA).Run(); err != nil {
620			return
621		}
622	
623		aheadCount, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-list", "--count", buildSHA+".."+headSHA)
624		if err != nil || aheadCount == "0" {
625			return
626		}
627	
628		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(),
629			"WARNING: installed ddx is built from %s; source tree HEAD is %s (%s commits ahead). Run \"make install\" to refresh.\n",
630			buildSHA, headSHA, aheadCount)
631	}

634		out, err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).Output()
635		if err != nil {
636			return "", err
637		}
638		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
639	}

641	func gitResolveCommit(dir, commit string) (string, error) {
642		return gitCommandOutput(dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", commit+"^{commit}")
643	}

645	func isDDXOriginURL(url string) bool {
646		return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(url), "documentdrivendx/ddx")
647	}

649	// resolveAgentSession looks up a session by ID from the agent session log.
650	// Returns nil if the session is not found or cannot be read.
651	func (f *CommandFactory) resolveAgentSession(sessionID string) *agent.SessionEntry {
652		if sessionID == "" {
653			return nil
654		}
655	
656		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(f.WorkingDir)
657		if err != nil {
658			return nil
659		}
660	
661		sessionLogDir := ".ddx/agent-logs"
662		if cfg.Agent != nil && cfg.Agent.SessionLogDir != "" {
663			sessionLogDir = cfg.Agent.SessionLogDir
664		}
665	
666		logDir := agent.ResolveLogDir(f.WorkingDir, sessionLogDir)
667		idx, ok, err := agent.FindSessionIndex(logDir, sessionID)
668		if err != nil {
669			return nil
670		}
671		if ok {
672			entry := agent.SessionIndexEntryToLegacy(idx)
673			bodies := agent.LoadSessionBodies(f.WorkingDir, idx)
674			entry.Prompt = bodies.Prompt
675			entry.Response = bodies.Response
676			entry.Stderr = bodies.Stderr
677			return &entry
678		}
679		return nil
680	}

31	const (

34		tryExitFailed          = 2 // execution_failed or any other terminal non-success

37	// newTryCommand creates the top-level "ddx try <bead-id>" command.
38	// It targets a single specific bead through the full production execution
39	// machinery (claim → execute → land → review), unlike "ddx work" which drains
40	// the queue by picker priority and cannot force a specific bead.
41	func (f *CommandFactory) newTryCommand() *cobra.Command {
42		cmd := &cobra.Command{
43			Use:          "try <bead-id>",
44			Short:        "Execute a specific bead through the production machinery",
45			SilenceUsage: true,
46			Long: `try executes a single named bead through the full production
47	execution machinery: claim → execute → land → review.
48	
49	Unlike "ddx work" which drains the queue by picker priority, "ddx try"
50	targets a specific bead ID. This is useful for:
51	  - Operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain
52	  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
53	    in dep order with an explicit ID)
54	  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time
55	
56	Pre-flight checks (run before any claim is issued):
57	  - bead not found → non-zero exit with "bead not found: <id>"
58	  - bead not claimable (status=closed, cancelled, etc.) → non-zero exit
59	  - bead has unmet dependencies → non-zero exit listing the blockers
60	
61	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
62	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
63	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
64	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
65	
66	Flag plumbing is identical to "ddx work": --harness, --model, --provider,
67	--profile, --effort are forwarded to the agent as opaque passthrough
68	constraints. DDx does not validate or branch on their string values.
69	
70	Exit codes:
71	  0  success or already_satisfied
72	  1  preserved_for_review or no_changes
73	  2  execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
74	`,
75			Example: `  # Execute a specific bead
76	  ddx try ddx-abc12345
77	
78	  # Execute with an explicit harness and model
79	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini
80	
81	  # Execute without the post-merge review step (break-glass only)
82	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing`,
83			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),

85		}

81	// FindNearestDDxWorkspace walks up from startDir to find the nearest ancestor
82	// inside the current git repository that contains a .ddx workspace.
83	//
84	// When startDir is inside a linked git worktree (e.g. an execute-bead
85	// isolated worktree under .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* or a worktrunk sibling
86	// like repo.feature-branch/), it resolves to the PRIMARY worktree's .ddx/
87	// first, not the linked worktree's own .ddx/. This is critical because:
88	//
89	//   - bead store mutations must land in the canonical project bead queue,
90	//     not in an isolated worktree's private snapshot that will be discarded
91	//   - the primary worktree is the operator's source of truth; linked
92	//     worktrees are ephemeral execution contexts
93	//
94	// If the primary worktree has no .ddx/ (or we aren't in a linked worktree),
95	// it falls back to walking up from startDir within the current git
96	// repository.
97	//
98	// Returns an empty string if no .ddx/ is found.
99	func FindNearestDDxWorkspace(startDir string) string {
100		abs, err := filepath.Abs(startDir)
101		if err != nil {
102			return ""
103		}
104	
105		// If we're inside a linked worktree, prefer the primary worktree's .ddx/.
106		if primary := primaryWorktreeRoot(abs); primary != "" && primary != FindProjectRoot(abs) {
107			candidate := filepath.Join(primary, ".ddx")
108			if info, statErr := os.Stat(candidate); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() {
109				return primary
110			}
111		}
112	
113		gitRoot := FindProjectRoot(abs)
114		current := abs
115		for {
116			candidate := filepath.Join(current, ".ddx")
117			if info, statErr := os.Stat(candidate); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() {
118				return current
119			}
120			if current == gitRoot {
121				break
122			}
123			parent := filepath.Dir(current)
124			if parent == current {
125				break
126			}
127			current = parent
128		}
129		return ""
130	}
131	
132	// IsRepository checks if the current directory is a git repository.
133	func IsRepository(path string) bool {
134		if path == "" {
135			path = "."
136		}
137	
138		cleanPath := filepath.Clean(path)
139		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
140		defer cancel()
141	
142		cmd := Command(ctx, cleanPath, "rev-parse", "--git-dir")
143		return cmd.Run() == nil
144	}
145	
146	// primaryWorktreeRoot returns the primary (non-linked) worktree directory
147	// for a given path inside a git repository, or "" if the path is not inside
148	// a linked worktree (or if resolution fails).
149	//
150	// Detection: `git rev-parse --git-common-dir` returns the shared .git
151	// directory. If that differs from `git rev-parse --git-dir`, we're in a
152	// linked worktree. The primary worktree is the parent directory of the
153	// common .git dir (unless the shared dir is a bare repo).
154	func primaryWorktreeRoot(startDir string) string {
155		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
156		defer cancel()
157	
158		gitDirCmd := Command(ctx, startDir, "rev-parse", "--path-format=absolute", "--git-dir")
159		gitDirOut, err := gitDirCmd.Output()
160		if err != nil {
161			return ""
162		}
163		gitDir := strings.TrimSpace(string(gitDirOut))
164	
165		commonDirCmd := Command(ctx, startDir, "rev-parse", "--path-format=absolute", "--git-common-dir")
166		commonDirOut, err := commonDirCmd.Output()
167		if err != nil {
168			return ""
169		}
170		commonDir := strings.TrimSpace(string(commonDirOut))
171	
172		if gitDir == commonDir {
173			// Not a linked worktree.
174			return ""
175		}
176	
177		// Common dir is either a bare repo or the primary worktree's .git dir.
178		// If it's a .git directory, the primary worktree is its parent.
179		if filepath.Base(commonDir) == ".git" {
180			return filepath.Dir(commonDir)
181		}
182		// Bare repo: no primary worktree. Caller falls back to walk-up.
183		return ""
184	}

## Are workers running for this project?

"Is the worker still working beads for this project?" "How is the queue
progressing?"

```bash
ddx work status            # live ddx work/ddx try processes for THIS project
ddx work status --json     # machine-readable
ddx work status --all-projects   # explicit cross-project escape hatch
```

`ddx work status` defaults to the current project root (CLI flag → env
`DDX_PROJECT_ROOT` → git root of CWD). The default view never reports
workers from another repository — surfacing a worker that belongs to a
different project as evidence that this project is "still working" is the
exact failure this command was added to prevent.

**Required when answering "is the worker still running for project X?":**

1. Resolve the requested project root before answering. Pass `--project`
   if you are not already in that root.
2. Use `ddx work status` (or `ddx work status --json`) for the live-worker
   evidence. Never substitute a raw global `ps aux | grep -E 'ddx work|ddx try'`
   scan: it returns workers from every checkout on the host and produces
   a misleading "yes, working" signal for whichever project happens to be
   asked.
3. Always name the project root the worker status applies to ("for
   `<project>`: …"). A bare "yes, a worker is running" answer is unsafe
   because the reader cannot tell which project it refers to.
4. If a live worker belongs to a *different* project, mention it only as
   explicitly out-of-scope context (`--all-projects` view), not as
   evidence that the asked-about project is progressing.

+		filepath.Join(".ddx", "agent-logs", "log.jsonl"): "{}\n",
+	}
+	for rel, content := range ignoredFiles {
+		path := filepath.Join(projectRoot, rel)
+		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755))
+		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0o644))
+	}
+
+	headBefore := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
+
+	committed, err := checkpointPreDispatchDirt(projectRoot, attemptID)
+	require.NoError(t, err)
+	assert.False(t, committed, "ignored generated paths should not create a checkpoint")
+	assert.Equal(t, headBefore, runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD"))
+}
+
+func TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirtyPathsExcludeIgnored(t *testing.T) {
+	projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
+
+	ignore := strings.Join([]string{
+		"cli/build/",
+		"website/public/",
+		".ddx/agent-logs/",
+	}, "\n") + "\n"
+	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".gitignore"), []byte(ignore), 0o644))
+	runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "add", ".gitignore")
+	runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "test: ignore generated paths")
+
+	ignoredPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, "cli", "build", "ddx")
+	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(ignoredPath), 0o755))
+	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(ignoredPath, []byte("binary"), 0o644))
+
+	implPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, "cli", "internal", "agent", "dirty_impl.go")
+	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(implPath), 0o755))
+	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(implPath, []byte("package agent\n"), 0o644))
+

root. The default scope matches CWD or the explicit `--project` flag;

an unfiltered global `ps | grep` scan that reports a different
project's worker.
EOF
